The Dark Tower (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $50,701,325Details
International Box Office $62,760,202
Worldwide Box Office $113,461,527
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,441,806
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,976,767
Total $12,418,573
Further financial details...

Synopsis

There are other worlds than these. The last Gunslinger, Roland Deschain, has been locked in an eternal battle with Walter O’Dim, also known as the Man in Black, determined to prevent him from toppling the Dark Tower, which holds the universe together. With the fate of the worlds at stake, good and evil will collide in the ultimate battle as only Roland can defend the Tower from the Man in Black.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,153,698 (37.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$66,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,451 opening theaters/3,451 max. theaters, 5.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $67,601,771

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 4th, 2017 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: August 2nd, 2017 (Wide) (Iceland)
August 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Iraq)
August 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Bahrain)
August 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Cambodia)
August 3rd, 2017 (Wide) (Egypt)
Video Release: October 17th, 2017 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material including sequences of gun violence and action.
(Rating bulletin 2485 (Cert #50333), 7/19/2017)
Running Time: 95 minutes
Franchise: The Dark Tower
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell
Plot point: Alternative Dimensions / Parallel universe, Cowboys in Modern Times, Good vs. Evil
Social setting: Post Apocalypse, Supernatural
Subgenre: Speculative Western
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Western
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Sony Pictures, MRC
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

There are other worlds than these. The last Gunslinger, Roland Deschain, has been locked in an eternal battle with Walter O’Dim, also known as the Man in Black, determined to prevent him from toppling the Dark Tower, which holds the universe together. With the fate of the worlds at stake, good and evil will collide in the ultimate battle as only Roland can defend the Tower from the Man in Black.
